violations.claim: When err is truthy, err.message || err always produces a truthy logged argument
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 590
; When err is truthy, err.message || err always produces a truthy logged argument
; Proof: preconditions + transitions + negated property → unsat
(declare-const err_truthy Int)           ; 1 = err is a truthy value
(declare-const err_message_truthy Int)   ; 1 = err.message is a truthy string
(declare-const logged_arg_truthy Int)    ; 1 = second argument to console.error is truthy
; Code transition: || selects err.message if truthy, otherwise falls back to err
(assert (=> (= err_message_truthy 1) (= logged_arg_truthy 1)))
(assert (=> (and (= err_message_truthy 0) (= err_truthy 1)) (= logged_arg_truthy 1)))
; Precondition: err is a truthy value (non-null, non-undefined, non-zero, non-empty)
(assert (= err_truthy 1))
; Negation: logged argument is falsy despite err being truthy
(assert (not (= logged_arg_truthy 1)))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: err: unknown — .message is accessed with no null/undefined guard
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 590
; err: unknown — .message is accessed with no null/undefined guard
; Any Promise rejection value reaches this handler; null and undefined are valid rejection values.
; Accessing .message on null or undefined throws TypeError, aborting the catch body
; before process.exit(1) is called.
(declare-const err_is_nullish Int)        ; 1 = err is null or undefined
(declare-const property_access_safe Int)  ; 1 = err.message does not throw
(declare-const exit_reached Int)          ; 1 = process.exit(1) is executed
; Code transition: JS property access on null/undefined throws TypeError
(assert (=> (= err_is_nullish 1) (= property_access_safe 0)))
; Code transition: TypeError in console.error(...) call aborts catch body before process.exit
(assert (=> (= property_access_safe 0) (= exit_reached 0)))
; Calling context: err is null — a valid Promise.reject(null) value; no guard in the handler
(assert (= err_is_nullish 1))
; Violation: process.exit(1) is never called — process exits via unhandled secondary TypeError
(assert (= exit_reached 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: Semantic: "Fatal:" label implies a human-readable error description is logged.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 590
; Semantic: "Fatal:" label implies a human-readable error description is logged.
; When err is a plain object with no .message property, err.message || err falls back
; to the raw object, which console.error serializes as "[object Object]".
(declare-const err_has_message_prop Int)     ; 1 = err.message is a non-empty string
(declare-const err_is_plain_object Int)      ; 1 = err is a plain JS object (not an Error)
(declare-const err_serializes_readable Int)  ; 1 = the fallback value is human-readable
(declare-const logged_is_descriptive Int)    ; 1 = console.error output describes the error
; Code transition: plain JS objects coerce to "[object Object]" in console output
(assert (=> (= err_is_plain_object 1) (= err_serializes_readable 0)))
; Code transition: err.message || err — when .message absent, err itself is the logged value
(assert (=> (and (= err_has_message_prop 0) (= err_serializes_readable 0))
            (= logged_is_descriptive 0)))
; Calling context: a plain object is thrown (e.g. throw {code: 404}); no .message property
(assert (= err_is_plain_object 1))
(assert (= err_has_message_prop 0))
; Violation: "Fatal: [object Object]" — the label promises diagnosis, the output provides none
(assert (= logged_is_descriptive 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: Error handler fragility: the catch handler is the program's last line of defence,
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 590
; Error handler fragility: the catch handler is the program's last line of defence,
; but it can itself throw before completing its two duties (log + exit).
; A handler that throws becomes a source of a new unhandled rejection,
; defeating the purpose of the catch entirely.
(declare-const handler_throws Int)         ; 1 = catch body throws before completion
(declare-const original_error_logged Int)  ; 1 = console.error("Fatal:") executes
(declare-const process_exits_cleanly Int)  ; 1 = process.exit(1) executes
; Code transition: if the handler body throws (e.g., err.message on null),
;                  neither console.error nor process.exit completes
(assert (=> (= handler_throws 1) (= original_error_logged 0)))
(assert (=> (= handler_throws 1) (= process_exits_cleanly 0)))
; Code transition: err is null — a reachable calling context (see P3 block above)
;                  causes the handler to throw a secondary TypeError
(assert (= handler_throws 1))
; Violation: the error handler fails to log AND fails to exit — both responsibilities unmet
(assert (and (= original_error_logged 0) (= process_exits_cleanly 0)))
(check-sat)
